_doc: Cross-language parity contract for the moderation pipeline's diff helpers.
Three implementations must agree: site/batch_annotate.py (_anchor_sig,
_preserve_human), site/moderate.py (_anchor_sig, finalize_for_post) and
wiki/src/validation.ts (anchorSig, deepEqual, findMatch, findLostHuman,
stampProvenance). Expected values were GENERATED BY RUNNING the current
implementations (pinned behavior, not aspirations). Runners:
wiki/test/parity.test.ts   (cd wiki && npx vitest run test/parity.test.ts)
site/test_parity.py        (python3 site/test_parity.py)
Case fields: name, fn (which helper family), inputs (a/b | target/pool |
stored/posted | existing/produced), expect, optional why / langs /
known_divergence. An expectation value of {'py': X, 'ts': Y} is
language-scoped: the languages GENUINELY disagree today; each side pins its
own value and the known_divergence field documents the finding. Fixing the
divergence in source requires updating the pin here — that is the point.
fn semantics per language:
anchor_sig  py: moderate._anchor_sig (and batch_annotate._anchor_sig,
asserted identical when the SDK is importable); ts: anchorSig.
expect.sig is always language-scoped (py json.dumps uses ', '
separators + ensure_ascii escapes; ts JSON.stringify is compact
and literal) — the strings never cross languages in production.
sig_equal   does sig(a) == sig(b) within each language.
deep_equal  py: dict == (the equality _preserve_human/finalize actually
use); ts: deepEqual.
find_match  ts-only (findMatch). py has no standalone twin; finalize
cases cover the py-side counterparts.
lost_human  ts-only (findLostHuman — the server 422).
stamp_provenance  ts-only (session-save stamping).
finalize    py-only execution (moderate.finalize_for_post); '<FRESH>' in
expect.out marks a minted 12-hex id. ts side cross-checks the
CONTRACT: the pinned output (fresh ids materialized) must pass
findLostHuman against existing — i.e. expect.lost_human_after.
preserve_human  py-only (batch_annotate._preserve_human; skipped when the
claude-agent-sdk import is unavailable).

cases.name: sig_basic_section_snippet
cases.why: The everyday shape: anchor {section, snippet}; type/value/from absent → null.

cases.name: sig_theorem_box
cases.why: Theorem-box anchors use {type, value}; section/snippet/from absent → null.

cases.name: sig_all_five_fields
cases.why: All five sig fields populated, pinning field order [type, section, snippet, value, from].

cases.name: sig_no_anchor_key
cases.why: No anchor at all → the all-null signature in both languages.

cases.name: sig_anchor_null
cases.why: anchor: null → treated as {} in both languages (py `or {}`, ts null-check).

cases.name: sig_unicode_emoji
cases.why: Unicode + astral-plane emoji. Python escapes (ensure_ascii), TS keeps literal chars — the sig STRINGS differ across languages but equality structure agrees.

cases.name: sig_very_long_snippet
cases.why: ~1.4k-char snippet; sigs are unbounded, no truncation in either language.

sig.py: [null, "Proof", "In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides \u2014 \u220e", null, null]
sig.ts: [null,"Proof","In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides — ∎",null,null]

cases.name: sig_falsy_values
cases.why: false/0/'' are preserved, not nulled: py .get returns them, ts ?? only catches null/undefined.

cases.name: sig_anchor_is_string
cases.why: A non-dict anchor value. Fix F12 (lockstep contract): Python no longer raises AttributeError — both languages guard the non-dict anchor and fall through to the anchors[]-then-all-null path (no anchors here → all-null).

cases.name: sig_anchor_is_array
cases.why: A non-empty array anchor. Fix F12: the singular `anchor` key is never read as a list (only the plural `anchors` key is) — non-dict anchor + no anchors → all-null in both languages, no crash.

cases.name: sig_anchor_empty_array
cases.why: EMPTY array anchor: non-dict → anchors[] fallback (absent) → all-null in both languages.

cases.name: sig_anchors_first_element_used
cases.why: F12 lockstep contract: no usable singular `anchor` + `anchors` a non-empty array whose first element is a plain object → the sig comes from anchors[0]'s [type, section, snippet, value, from]; later elements are ignored.

cases.name: sig_anchor_dict_wins_over_anchors
cases.why: F12 lockstep contract: a plain-dict `anchor` keeps its UNCHANGED pre-fix behavior — anchors[] is only consulted when the singular anchor is not a plain object.

cases.name: sig_non_dict_anchor_falls_back_to_anchors
cases.why: F12 lockstep contract: a non-dict `anchor` (here a string) must not crash and falls through to anchors[0] when that is a plain object.

cases.name: sig_anchors_first_element_not_object
cases.why: F12 lockstep contract: anchors[] whose FIRST element is not a plain object (string here) → all-null signature; only anchors[0] is ever considered, never a later element.

cases.name: sig_anchors_empty_array
cases.why: F12 lockstep contract: an EMPTY anchors[] is not usable → all-null signature.

cases.name: sigeq_identical_anchor
cases.why: Same anchor, different non-anchor fields → equal sigs (sig covers anchor only).

cases.name: sigeq_missing_vs_explicit_null
cases.why: Missing fields and explicit nulls both serialize as null → equal sigs.

cases.name: sigeq_extra_anchor_key_ignored
cases.why: Keys outside [type, section, snippet, value, from] don't feed the sig.

cases.name: sigeq_anchors_plural_now_read
cases.why: F12 (was sigeq_anchors_plural_not_read, which pinned the plural key being IGNORED): anchors[0] now feeds the sig, so a plural-key payload no longer collides with anchorless annotations.

cases.name: sigeq_anchor_vs_anchors_same_payload
cases.why: F12: a singular anchor and an anchors[0] carrying the same five fields produce EQUAL sigs — the two wire shapes are interchangeable for matching.

cases.name: sigeq_numeric_int_vs_float
cases.why: JSON wire: '1' parses to int in py / number in ts; '1.0' parses to float in py / the SAME number in ts.
cases.known_divergence: py json.dumps renders 1 vs 1.0 distinctly → sigs DIFFER; ts has one number type → sigs EQUAL. site/batch_annotate.py:284 vs wiki/src/validation.ts:55. Only reachable with non-string anchor field values (real anchors are strings).

cases.name: sigeq_numeric_vs_string
cases.why: Number 1 vs string '1' → different sigs in BOTH languages (types survive JSON).

cases.name: sigeq_nested_object_key_order
cases.why: A (pathological) object-valued anchor field with permuted key order.
cases.known_divergence: py sort_keys=True canonicalizes nested dicts → sigs EQUAL; ts JSON.stringify preserves insertion order → sigs DIFFER. The validation.ts:50 comment 'sort_keys is moot — entries are scalars' holds only for scalar anchor fields. site/batch_annotate.py:284-285 vs wiki/src/validation.ts:55-61.

cases.name: deq_key_order_insensitive
cases.why: Key order never matters — py dict == and ts deepEqual both key-set-based.

cases.name: deq_extra_key_moderation_note
cases.why: LOAD-BEARING for the 422: key-set strictness means _preserve_human's moderation_note (or any agent-added field) breaks deep equality, which is exactly why finalize_for_post must post the stored human verbatim.

cases.name: deq_extra_key_moderation_flag
cases.why: Same 422 pin for the MODERATE_AGENT1_SYSTEM-sanctioned moderation_flag field.

cases.name: deq_null_vs_absent
cases.why: Explicit null and absent key are NOT deep-equal in either language (key-set strictness).

cases.name: deq_bool_vs_int
cases.why: JSON true vs JSON 1.
cases.known_divergence: Python == says True == 1 → dicts EQUAL; ts deepEqual uses === → NOT equal. In Python this equality is only used for ladder/wire STATS counting (batch_annotate.py:306, moderate.py:140), never for output content, so the blast radius is a miscounted stat. wiki/src/validation.ts:66-77.

cases.name: deq_int_vs_float
cases.why: Agreement, but for different reasons: ts JSON-parses both to the same number; py int/float compare equal cross-type. Pinned so neither side drifts.

cases.name: deq_provenance_only_differs
cases.why: A bare provenance flip IS a deep change — which is why stampProvenance strips provenance before judging 'changed' (anti-laundering).

cases.name: fm_by_id_sig_differs
cases.why: ids, once present on both sides, are authoritative: id match wins even when the anchor moved.

cases.name: fm_both_ids_disagree_same_sig
cases.why: Same anchor but BOTH sides carry ids that disagree → sig match rejected.
cases.known_divergence: Python finalize_for_post's human-matching falls back to anchor-sig even when ids disagree (moderate.py:132-134 h_by_sig lookup has no id-disagreement guard) — see fz_human_sig_match_despite_id_disagreement for why the wire outcome still converges. wiki/src/validation.ts:88-92.

cases.name: fm_target_id_pool_no_id
cases.why: Only one side has an id → sig match allowed.

cases.name: fm_target_no_id_pool_has_id
cases.why: The heal-adopts-stored-id seam: a posted annotation without an id sig-matches the stored one, so healAnnotationIds (index.ts) adopts the stored id — the TS twin of finalize_for_post's ids_inherited (fz_missing_id_inherits).

cases.name: fm_id_beats_sig
cases.why: Precedence: the by-id match (pool[1]) wins over the sig twin (pool[0]).

cases.name: fm_numeric_id_honored
cases.why: annId accepts numbers.
cases.known_divergence: TS findMatch honors numeric ids (validation.ts:42-44) while Python finalize_for_post treats every non-string id as absent (moderate.py:129 isinstance check) — see fz_numeric_id_treated_as_absent. The Worker save path 400s numeric ids before any matching (ids.test.ts pins 42 → 'invalid annotation id'), so the wire never carries one.

cases.name: fm_empty_string_id_ignored
cases.why: '' is not an id in either language (annId returns undefined; py truthiness).

cases.name: lh_human_echoed_keyorder_permuted
cases.why: Key order doesn't trip the 422 (deepEqual is key-order-insensitive).

cases.name: lh_tombstone_dropped
cases.why: Tombstones are human vetoes: dropping one (the resurrection precondition) is a 422 violation like any other human loss.

cases.name: lh_tombstone_unrejected
cases.why: Flipping a tombstone's status back (in-place resurrection) alters a stored human annotation → 422.

cases.name: lh_ai_dropped_ok
cases.why: Only provenance='human' rows are guarded; AI annotations may be dropped freely.

cases.name: lh_extra_key_moderation_note
cases.why: THE seam finalize_for_post exists for: _preserve_human's moderation_note would 422 if posted, so the wire pass strips it by posting the stored original.

cases.name: lh_unlabeled_human_dropped
cases.why: No label → the violation is reported as the TS anchor-sig string (pinned verbatim, ts formatting).

cases.name: lh_empty_label_falls_back_to_sig
cases.why: Empty-string label also falls back to the sig string.

cases.name: lh_id_disagreement_defeats_sig
cases.why: The posted twin carries a different id → findMatch rejects the sig match → 422 even though content is otherwise identical. Bots must echo ids verbatim.

cases.name: lh_bot_added_id_to_stored_human
cases.why: Contract ID2: a stored human annotation without an id must not gain one from a bot write — the added key breaks deep equality → 422 (sig match still found since the stored side has no id). Id backfill for humans is SQL-only.

cases.name: sp_flip_ai_to_human_reverted
cases.why: Anti-laundering direction 1: a bare flip cannot launder ai work into human credit — judged with provenance stripped, unchanged → stored provenance kept.

cases.name: sp_flip_human_to_ai_reverted
cases.why: Anti-laundering direction 2: a bare flip cannot disown human provenance either.

cases.name: sp_real_change_forces_human
cases.why: A session save that actually changes content is stamped human regardless of what provenance the client sent.

cases.name: sp_new_annotation_forced_human
cases.why: No stored match → forced human (session saves can only mint human work).

cases.name: fz_echoed_id_survives
cases.why: Known id echoed verbatim → survives; the agent's non-human edits stand.

cases.name: fz_unknown_id_inherits_by_sig
cases.why: Contract ID1: an id the store never issued is discarded; the anchor-sig twin's stored id is inherited (renaming ids cannot fork identity).

cases.name: fz_missing_id_inherits
cases.why: The py twin of the Worker's heal-adopts-stored-id (fm_target_no_id_pool_has_id).

cases.name: fz_new_gets_fresh
cases.why: A genuinely new annotation gets a fresh 12-hex id.

cases.name: fz_human_altered_restored_wire
cases.why: Wire-level human preservation: the stored human is posted byte-identical, so the pinned output must pass findLostHuman (ts cross-check).

cases.name: fz_human_dropped_reinserted
cases.why: A dropped human is re-appended verbatim — the 422 made impossible client-side.

cases.name: fz_tombstone_resurrection_replaced
cases.why: A same-anchor resurrection of a human veto is REPLACED by the stored tombstone (sig match → stored human posted verbatim). Shifted-anchor near-misses are dropped by the F6 veto-adjacency check — see fz_veto_adjacent_near_miss_dropped.

cases.name: fz_veto_adjacent_near_miss_dropped
cases.why: F6: a produced NEW annotation (no stored counterpart by id or sig) in a stored tombstone's section whose snippet contains >50% of the tombstone snippet's word tokens is DROPPED at the wire (veto_adjacent_dropped) — shifted-anchor resurrections of a human veto no longer reach the server. The dropped human/tombstone is still re-appended verbatim, so the ts findLostHuman cross-check passes.

cases.name: fz_anti_laundering_downgrade
cases.why: provenance='human' without a stored human twin is downgraded to ai-moderated: bot writes cannot mint human provenance (client twin of stampProvenance).

cases.name: fz_numeric_id_treated_as_absent
cases.why: Non-string ids are not ids to finalize_for_post → inherit-by-sig.
cases.known_divergence: Pair of fm_numeric_id_honored: TS findMatch would match id 7 by identity; py discards it and inherits the stored string id. Convergent on the wire only because finalize replaces the id BEFORE posting and the Worker 400s numeric ids it ever sees.

cases.name: fz_human_sig_match_despite_id_disagreement
cases.why: Pair of fm_both_ids_disagree_same_sig + lh_id_disagreement_defeats_sig: python matches the stored human BY SIG despite the disagreeing id and posts the stored original (id restored), so the server-side id-strict findMatch never sees the disagreement. Function-level divergence, wire-level convergence — pinned from both ends.

cases.name: ph_dropped_reinserted_with_note
cases.why: The reinserted human gains the pinned moderation_note string — which is why finalize_for_post must strip it before the wire (lh_extra_key_moderation_note).

cases.name: ph_offsig_resurrection_survives
cases.why: REALITY PIN: _preserve_human only suppresses EXACT-sig resurrections — a re-annotation at a shifted snippet survives THIS pass alongside the re-appended tombstone. Since F6, finalize_for_post drops such near-misses at the wire (fz_veto_adjacent_near_miss_dropped pins the same inputs); a different-section or <=50%-containment re-annotation remains guarded only by the agent prompt (rule 5).

cases.name: ph_id_match_reanchored_replaced_flag_harvested
cases.why: F7: the agent re-anchored a human annotation (sig no longer matches) — the id match REPLACES the altered copy in place, so exactly ONE copy survives (no ai-moderated duplicate next to a re-appended human). F14: the moderation_flag the agent attached is harvested into stats.flags as [id, flag] before the restore strips it.
